MAGS ETF: Two Signals Will Determine If the 16% Slide Gets Worse in 2026. Tesla’s Robotaxi service found a home in this East Bay city. Tesla's Robotaxi ride-hailing service has established a new hub in downtown Oakland, utilizing the parking garage of the recently completed 1900 Broadway apartment tower by Behring Companies. This marks the second such location in the East Bay for Tesla's service, which launched in the Bay Area last year.

Tesla scaling up in Japan with plans to double service centers, more showrooms (TSLA:NASDAQ). Tesla is set to expand its presence in Japan by doubling its service centers and opening more physical showrooms. This strategic move aims to capture a larger share of the world's third-largest automotive market. The expansion indicates a pivot towards more physical customer touchpoints and increased staffing in the region.

Target Corporation $TGT Stock Position Lowered by Private Advisory Group LLC. Private Advisory Group LLC reduced its stake in Target Corporation (TGT) by 12.7% in Q4 2025, selling over 14,800 shares. Despite this, Target outperformed Q4 EPS expectations, issued optimistic FY2026 guidance, and declared a quarterly dividend. Institutional ownership remains high, though analysts currently rate the stock as a "Hold."

LMC: Target opens at West Orange Plaza to continue retail center’s transformation. A new Target store has opened in West Orange Plaza, marking a significant milestone in the shopping center's ongoing transformation. Occupying 150,000 square feet, this is Target's 55th New Jersey location and is part of a broader redevelopment project by Levin Management Corp. The opening is expected to create 280 jobs and features various services and in-store offerings, further enhancing the plaza's appeal as a retail destination alongside other new tenants.

Target Opens New Location at West Orange Plaza. Target has opened its 55th store in New Jersey, a 150,000-square-foot location at West Orange Plaza, marking one of three new stores in the state within the past year. The new store, which features a CVS Pharmacy, Starbucks, and Target Optical, is expected to employ around 280 people and offers services like drive-up and same-day delivery. This opening is part of a broader multimillion-dollar redevelopment of West Orange Plaza, aiming to enhance the shopping experience and attract more tenants.

Reduce Drinkware, Vera Bradley Debut Limited-Edition Collection at Target. Reduce Drinkware and Vera Bradley have launched a limited-edition collection of tumblers and accessories, exclusively available at Target. The collaboration, facilitated by IMG Licensing, features Vera Bradley's signature patterns on Reduce's reusable drinkware. Both brands emphasize functionality and joy, appealing to a multi-generational audience.

DIS: The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) Given Consensus Rating of "Moderate Buy" by Analysts. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) has received a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with an average 12-month price target of $134.00. Institutional investors have significantly increased their holdings in the company, showcasing strong confidence. Despite positive trends in streaming profitability and theme park expansion, concerns remain regarding tech partnership setbacks and analyst caution.

Scoop: In 2024, Disney considered building hotels inside the Magic Kingdom, records show. Court records reveal that in early 2024, Disney considered building one or two new hotels inside the Magic Kingdom as part of a major expansion, a revelation made during a deposition by a Disney Imagineering executive. While this idea was reportedly nixed by August 2024, the concept highlights Disney's strategic planning amidst its legal battles with Florida Republicans and its investment in theme parks. Experts suggest such a hotel would be extremely popular, though its success would depend on ideal location, theme, and pricing.

The Walt Disney Company Stock: Navigating Entertainment Challenges and Valuation Opportunities in 20. The Walt Disney Company's stock is navigating entertainment challenges, including streaming shifts and market volatility, but valuation metrics suggest potential undervaluation. The company's diversified segments—Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences—are key drivers of value, with the Experiences segment particularly strong in North America. While macroeconomic pressures and competition pose risks, strategic initiatives in streaming, content optimization, and parks expansion position Disney for long-term growth and make it an attractive consideration for North American investors.

The Walt Disney Company Stock: Navigating Entertainment Shifts and Investor Opportunities in a Strea. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) is navigating the evolving entertainment landscape by focusing on its diversified portfolio, including entertainment studios, ESPN, and theme parks. Strategic pivots towards streaming profitability, resilience in its park division, and leveraging its vast intellectual property are key to its sustained investor interest. Despite competitive pressures and market shifts, analysts lean positive on Disney's growth potential due to its core strengths and adaptability.

The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection Names SVP, Customer Experience. The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection has appointed Tim Klauda as Senior Vice President of Customer Experience, a new role effective immediately. Klauda will be responsible for enhancing the guest experience across the fleet and all touchpoints, ensuring alignment with The Ritz-Carlton's core values. He brings extensive leadership experience from Royal Caribbean, McDonald's, and Disney, among others.

Nike Q3 Preview: 'Maybe There Is Hope,' Says Expert After Stock Hits 9-Year Low. Nike Inc. (NKE) is set to release its third-quarter financial results, with analysts expecting a decline in EPS and revenue year-over-year. Despite the stock hitting a nine-year low, market expert Jay Woods suggests there could be hope for a rebound, noting the stock has rallied after two of its last three earnings reports. Key areas to watch include China sales, tariff commentary, and the impact of recent layoffs, as investors look for signs of a turnaround.
